div.h3: Goal: Accumulate 768 products without O(N) float32 drift

div.p: c tracks the lost precision from the previous addition. By subtracting c from the current input, we "re-inject" the lost bits back into the flow before they are truncated again.
sum is 10^3, y is ~10^-6. The 23-bit mantissa of an f32 cannot hold both. Adding y causes its lower bits to shift right and drop. t becomes exactly 1000.0000.
Algebraically, (sum + y) - sum - y should be 0. In floating point:

ol.li: (t - sum) recovers the actual increment applied.
Subtracting y from that increment reveals the lost part.

div.p: We store the trial sum. Even though sum is "missing" the bits, the variable c now holds them for the next loop.
Error: O(N * ε) Loss: ~5.1e-4 [#######---] Bits lost: 7-9
Error: O(ε) Loss: ~1.2e-7 [##########] Full precision maintained
